# Echelon Analytics: Full Documentation

> Privacy-first, self-hosted, cookieless web analytics. Single script tag, single SQLite database. AGPL-3.0.

Repository: https://github.com/janit/ea
Website: https://ea.js.org

---

# Installation

## Prerequisites

- Deno 2.x (includes SQLite via node:sqlite)
- No external database — single SQLite file, zero config

## Quick Start

```
git clone https://github.com/janit/ea.git
cd ea/echelon-analytics
deno task dev
```

The dev server starts on http://localhost:1947 with Vite HMR.

## Add the Tracker

```html
<script async src="https://your-host/ea.js" data-site="my-site"></script>
```

Pageviews, bounces, and sessions are tracked automatically.

## Development Commands

All commands run from echelon-analytics/:

```
deno task dev        # Development server (Vite HMR)
deno task build      # Production build
deno task start      # Start production server (must build first)
deno task check      # Format, lint, type-check, and run tests
deno task test       # Run server-side tests (unit + integration)
deno task test:e2e   # Run browser E2E tests (requires Chromium)
deno fmt --check .   # Format check
deno lint .          # Lint
deno check main.ts   # Type-check
deno task update     # Update Fresh framework
```

## Docker

Multi-stage build with non-root user and health check:

```
docker build -f confs/Dockerfile -t echelon-analytics .
docker run -p 1947:1947 -v echelon-data:/app/data echelon-analytics
```

The container exposes port 1947. Mount a volume at /app/data/ for the SQLite database.

- Base image: denoland/deno:2.7.12
- Uses tini for proper PID 1 signal handling
- Non-root user echelon (UID 1001)
- Health check: GET /api/health every 30s

## Reverse Proxy

The application already sets CSP, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options on HTML responses. Do not duplicate those in your reverse proxy — it causes double headers. The proxy should add HSTS, Permissions-Policy, and strip the Server header. See confs/Caddyfile.example for a complete configuration.

### Caddy

```
echelon.example.com {
    reverse_proxy localhost:1947
    encode gzip zstd
    header {
        Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"
        Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin
        Permissions-Policy "geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()"
        -Server
    }
}
```

### Nginx

```
server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name echelon.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:1947;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }

    # Headers the app does NOT set — safe to add here.
    # Do NOT add CSP, X-Frame-Options, or X-Content-Type-Options
    # (the app already sets those on HTML responses).
    add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" always;
    add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
    add_header Permissions-Policy "geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()" always;
    server_tokens off;

    gzip on;
    gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json application/javascript text/xml image/svg+xml;
}
```

Set ECHELON_TRUST_PROXY=true when behind a reverse proxy.

## Cloudflare

```
ECHELON_BEHIND_CLOUDFLARE=true
ECHELON_TRUST_PROXY=true
```

## Authentication Setup

### API Token

```
ECHELON_SECRET=your-secret-token-here
```

Use with Authorization: Bearer your-secret-token-here header.

### Username/Password

```
cd echelon-analytics
deno eval "import{hashPassword}from'./lib/auth.ts';console.log(await hashPassword('yourpassword'))"

ECHELON_USERNAME=admin
ECHELON_PASSWORD_HASH=pbkdf2$600000$<salt>$<hash>
```

Both auth modes can be used simultaneously.

## Single-Process Constraint

All state is in memory (sessions, rate limiter, burst maps, buffered writers). Must run with a single Deno worker — do not use --parallel with deno serve.

## Public Mode

Set ECHELON_PUBLIC_MODE=true for a read-only public dashboard:

- No authentication required — anyone can view all admin pages
- All mutations blocked — POST, PATCH, DELETE return 403 with error "read_only"
- Mutation controls (forms, delete buttons) hidden in admin UI
- Internal stats redacted (buffer sizes, RPS, uptime) from API responses
- robots.txt switches to Allow with Sitemap directive
- sitemap.xml includes all admin page URLs
- /api/health and POST /api/telemetry remain open (exempt from write block)

Use for public demo dashboards. The live instance at ea.islets.app runs in public mode with anonymized data.

## Security Hardening

### CSRF Protection

Cookie-authenticated mutating requests (POST, PATCH, DELETE) require the Origin or Referer header's host to match the request Host header. Compares hosts (not full origins) so it works behind reverse proxies that change the scheme.

### PoW Nonce Tracking

Solved proof-of-work tokens are tracked in an in-memory Set. Replaying a previously-used token adds penalty points (+40) to the bot score instead of passing verification. Nonces are evicted when the challenge window expires (default 10 minutes + 1 minute grace period).

### Response Headers

All HTML responses include security headers:

- Content-Security-Policy (CSP) with script-src, style-src, connect-src, img-src directives
- X-Frame-Options: DENY (prevents clickjacking)
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff (prevents MIME sniffing)
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

### Session Security

- PBKDF2-SHA256 password hashing with 600,000 iterations
- Random session tokens (crypto.randomUUID)
- 24-hour absolute TTL + 30-minute idle timeout
- Sessions pruned from memory every 30 minutes
- Login rate-limited per IP (5 attempts per 15-minute window)
- Prior sessions invalidated on new login

### LIKE Query Escaping

Admin search queries (visitors, events) escape % and _ characters in user input to prevent LIKE injection in SQL queries.

## Anonymization

Sites listed in ECHELON_ANONYMIZE_SITES get deterministic anonymization applied before storage — original values never touch disk:

- Visitor IDs: HMAC-SHA256 hashed with a key derived from ECHELON_SECRET + current date. Same visitor on the same day always maps to the same anonymous ID.
- Session IDs: Mapped to Norwegian fisherman names (e.g. "Torstein Sjømann", "Olav Sildekong")
- Country codes: Replaced with fictional exoplanet names (e.g. "Kepler-442b", "Proxima-b")
- Screen sizes: Mapped to classic terminal resolutions (e.g. 1920x1080 → 80×25 IBM PC)
- OS names: Mapped to tropical bird names (e.g. "macOS 10.15" → "Scarlet Macaw")
- Device types: Mapped to sci-fi vessel classes (desktop → mothership, tablet → shuttle, mobile → probe)
- Referrers: Replaced with fictional NSA intranet URLs (e.g. https://nsa-intranet.gov/ops/PRISM-a3f2)
- UTM params: Mapped to military operation codenames (e.g. "operation-mockingbird")
- Experiments: Hashed to experiment-<hash> / variant-<hash>
- Event data: Sanitized to safe behavioral keys only per event type (scroll depth, dwell time, metric values, HTML tag names). URLs, user text, and custom attributes are stripped.
- Form field values: Scrambled client-side before transmission (each letter replaced with random letter, each digit with random digit)
- URL query parameters: Query param values scrambled client-side to prevent search text from appearing in page paths

All mappings are deterministic within a day (same input → same output). Daily key rotation ensures anonymized IDs change each day.

The public demo dashboard at ea.islets.app uses this to display real traffic patterns without exposing any visitor information.

## Telemetry

Opt-in anonymous usage tracking (no visitor data, PII, or page content). An admin UI banner lets operators opt in or out. The setting is stored in the database (site_settings table) and can be overridden with ECHELON_TELEMETRY=true or ECHELON_TELEMETRY=false environment variable.

See https://ea.js.org/telemetry.html for details.

---

# Features

## Script Tag

```html
<script async src="https://your-host/ea.js" data-site="my-site"></script>
```

## Script Attributes

All behavioral tracking (clicks, scroll, hover, outbound, downloads, forms, vitals) is enabled by default. Use data-no-* attributes to opt out of specific features.

| Attribute | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| data-site | Site identifier. Defaults to "default" if omitted. |
| data-cookie | Enable returning-visitor cookie (opt-in). With ECHELON_COOKIE_CONSENT=true, shows consent banner first. |
| data-no-clicks | Disable click tracking on elements with data-echelon-click attribute. |
| data-no-scroll | Disable scroll depth milestones (25%, 50%, 75%, 90%, 100%). |
| data-no-hover | Disable 1-second hover tracking on elements with data-echelon-hover attribute. |
| data-no-outbound | Disable outbound link click tracking. |
| data-no-downloads | Disable file download link tracking (pdf, zip, dmg, mp3, mp4, etc.). |
| data-no-forms | Disable form tracking (field focus, edits, submissions). |
| data-no-vitals | Disable Core Web Vitals: LCP, CLS, INP. |

### Selective Opt-Out

```html
<script async src="https://your-host/ea.js"
  data-site="my-site"
  data-no-hover
  data-no-vitals></script>
```

## HTML Element Markup

### Click Tracking (on by default, disable with data-no-clicks)

```html
<button data-echelon-click="signup-cta">Sign Up</button>
```

### Hover Tracking (on by default, disable with data-no-hover, fires after 1s)

```html
<div data-echelon-hover="pricing-card">...</div>
```

### Form Tracking (on by default, disable with data-no-forms)

Tracks three levels of form interaction:

- form_focus: User focuses an input, select, or textarea. Data: tag, input type, field name, form ID/name, path.
- form_blur: User edits a field (fires on change/blur). Data: same as focus + value length and value (non-sensitive fields only; passwords/hidden excluded). On anonymized sites, values are scrambled client-side.
- form_submit: Form submission. Data: method, ID, name, optional label, path.

Optionally label forms:

```html
<form data-echelon-form="contact-form">...</form>
```

### Custom Data Attributes

Any data-echelon-* attribute on annotated elements is included in event data (max 8 keys, max 256 chars per value).

```html
<button data-echelon-click="buy"
  data-echelon-product="widget"
  data-echelon-price="9.99">Buy Now</button>
```

## JavaScript API

```js
window.echelon.track("event_name", {
  key: "value",
  another: "prop"
});
```

Limits: max 16 properties, key max 64 chars, value max 512 chars.

## Auto-Tracked Events

| Event | Trigger |
|-------|---------|
| pageview | Via /b.gif beacon after first user interaction (pointer, scroll, click, keydown) or on visibility change after 4 seconds. Minimum 800ms interaction delay. |
| bounce | No interaction within 120 seconds, or tab hidden without prior interaction. |
| session_end | Tab becomes hidden. |
| session_resume | Tab becomes visible again after session_end. |
| form_focus | User focuses a form field (input, select, textarea). |
| form_blur | User edits a form field (fires on change/blur). |
| form_submit | Form submission. |

## SPA Support

The tracker automatically patches pushState, replaceState, and listens for popstate events. When the route changes, a new pageview beacon fires automatically. Works with React Router, Vue Router, SvelteKit, Fresh, or any other SPA framework.

## UTM Campaign Tracking

Echelon tracks all five standard UTM parameters. Attribution is session-scoped and persisted across page loads.

### Supported Parameters

| Parameter | Purpose | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| utm_campaign | Campaign name / ID | spring-2026 |
| utm_source | Traffic source | newsletter, google |
| utm_medium | Marketing medium | email, cpc, social |
| utm_content | Ad creative / content variant | hero-banner, sidebar-link |
| utm_term | Search keyword / term | analytics+tool |

### How It Works

1. Visitor arrives with UTM parameters in the URL (e.g. ?utm_campaign=spring-2026&utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email)
2. Tracker extracts all five params and stores them in sessionStorage (key: _eutm)
3. Every pageview beacon (/b.gif) includes the UTM parameters for the rest of the session — even on pages without UTM in the URL
4. SPA navigation: on route changes (pushState/replaceState), tracker re-reads URL params. New utm_campaign updates the session; otherwise stored values persist
5. Server validates: the beacon endpoint checks utm_campaign against a whitelist of active campaigns (refreshed from DB every 60 seconds). Unknown campaigns are silently dropped

### Setting Up Campaigns

Campaigns must be registered before they appear in stats:

API:
```
POST /api/campaigns
{ "id": "spring-2026", "name": "Spring Launch 2026", "utm_campaign": "spring-2026", "site_id": "default" }
```

Or use the form at /admin/campaigns. Fields: id (URL-safe slug), name, utm_campaign (exact URL value), site_id.

### Campaign Lifecycle

- active: UTM data is recorded, appears in stats
- paused: New UTM data silently dropped, existing data remains in stats
- archived: Same as paused, hidden from default campaign list

Change status: PATCH /api/campaigns/:id with { "status": "paused" }

### Campaign Stats

GET /api/stats/campaigns?id=X returns:
- Summary: total views and unique visitors
- By Source: breakdown by utm_source (views + visitors)
- By Medium: breakdown by utm_medium
- By Content: breakdown by utm_content
- By Term: breakdown by utm_term
- Daily Trend: views per day over selected period
- Top Landing Pages: pages visitors entered through

All stats exclude bot traffic (bot_score >= 50). Default lookback 30 days (max 90).

### UTM URL Format

```
https://example.com/sale?utm_campaign=spring-2026&utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_content=hero-cta
```

All parameters optional except utm_campaign, which must match an active campaign. The whitelist prevents spam traffic from polluting stats — unknown utm_campaign values are silently dropped.

## A/B Experiments

Run split tests to measure the impact of changes. Echelon tracks impressions, conversions, and calculates statistical significance automatically.

### How It Works

1. Create an experiment with variants and a conversion metric (event type to measure)
2. Your application assigns visitors to variants and sends events with experiment attribution
3. Echelon counts unique sessions per variant (impressions) and sessions that triggered the metric event (conversions)
4. Statistical significance is calculated using a two-proportion z-test

### Creating an Experiment

API:
```
POST /api/experiments
{
  "experiment_id": "checkout-v2",
  "name": "Checkout Redesign",
  "description": "Test new checkout flow vs original",
  "metric_event_type": "purchase",
  "allocation_percent": 100,
  "variants": [
    { "variant_id": "control", "name": "Original", "weight": 50, "is_control": true },
    { "variant_id": "redesign", "name": "New Flow", "weight": 50 }
  ]
}
```

Or use the form at /admin/experiments. Max 20 variants. Each variant has an optional config field (JSON, max 4096 chars).

### Variant Weights

Weights are relative, not percentages. Two variants with weights 50/50 get 50% each. Weights 1/1 produce the same split. Three variants 2/1/1 get 50%/25%/25%.

Your application reads weights and assigns visitors (e.g. hash of visitor ID modulo total weight). Echelon stores weights but does not perform assignment.

### Sending Experiment Events

Include experiment_id and variant_id in event data:

```js
// Track impression
window.echelon.track("page_view", { experiment_id: "checkout-v2", variant_id: "redesign" });

// Track conversion
window.echelon.track("purchase", { experiment_id: "checkout-v2", variant_id: "redesign" });
```

Impressions = unique sessions that sent any event for experiment+variant.
Conversions = unique sessions that sent the metric_event_type event.

### Experiment Lifecycle

- draft: Created but not yet active. Events still recorded if sent.
- active: Running. Sets started_at on first activation.
- paused: Temporarily stopped. Events still recorded.
- completed: Finished. Sets ended_at. Results frozen.
- archived: Same as completed, hidden from default views.

Change status: PATCH /api/experiments/:id with { "status": "active" }

### Results

GET /api/stats/experiments?experiment_id=X returns per-variant:
- Impressions: unique sessions enrolled
- Conversions: unique sessions that triggered metric event
- Conversion Rate: conversions / impressions
- Relative Uplift: (variant_rate - control_rate) / control_rate (non-control only)
- Significance: statistical significance level

### Statistical Significance

Uses a two-proportion z-test:
1. Pooled proportion: p = (c_control + c_variant) / (n_control + n_variant)
2. Standard error: SE = sqrt(p * (1-p) * (1/n1 + 1/n2))
3. Z-score: z = |rate_variant - rate_control| / SE

| Z-Score | Significance |
|---------|-------------|
| >= 2.576 | 99% significant (p < 0.01) |
| >= 1.960 | 95% significant (p < 0.05) |
| >= 1.645 | 90% significant (p < 0.10) |
| < 1.645 | Not statistically significant |

Minimum data: 100 impressions per variant required before significance is calculated.

## Cookieless by Default

Without data-cookie, visitors are identified by a daily-rotating HMAC-SHA256 hash of IP + User-Agent + site ID + date. This produces a 16-character hex identifier that:

- Resets every day — no cross-day tracking
- Cannot be reversed to recover the original IP
- Requires no cookie consent (GDPR/ePrivacy compliant)

Add data-cookie to enable a persistent visitor cookie for returning visitor tracking.

## Admin Dashboard

The admin UI at /admin/ provides a full-featured analytics dashboard:

- Live dashboard with Now gauge, 60-minute and 24-hour trend charts, recent visitors/events (auto-refreshes every 10s)
- Overview with KPIs, daily trends, top pages, devices, countries, referrers
- Visitor views listing — filterable, sortable, paginated
- Visitor detail pages with full history, events, bot scores, exclude/include controls
- Semantic events listing with type badges and filtering
- Realtime visitor monitoring (10-second polling)
- Bot management — suspicious visitors, exclude/include controls
- A/B experiments — create, manage, view conversion uplift
- UTM campaigns — track and break down by source, medium, content
- Performance metrics — CI/CD benchmark tracking with trends
- Professional design with navy and burgundy palette
- Persistent site selector and date range picker in navigation
- Configurable timezone display for all admin timestamps (ECHELON_DISPLAY_TIMEZONE)

---

# API Reference

## Authentication

All API endpoints (except /api/health) require one of:

- Bearer token: Authorization: Bearer <ECHELON_SECRET>
- Session cookie: echelon_session from password login

Cookie-authenticated POST/PATCH/DELETE requests require CSRF validation (Origin or Referer header must match the server host).

## Public Endpoints

GET /ea.js — Returns the tracker script with embedded WASM proof-of-work challenge.
GET /b.gif — Pageview beacon. Returns a 1x1 transparent GIF.
POST /e — Behavioral events endpoint (via sendBeacon). Max 16 KB body, max 20 events per request. Returns 204.
GET /api/health — Returns { "status": "ok", "service": "Echelon Analytics" }.

## Stats

GET /api/stats/overview?site_id=X&days=N — Overview statistics. days defaults to 30 (max 730).
GET /api/stats/dashboard?site_id=X — Live dashboard data: active visitors (now), 60-min trend (per-minute), 24h trend (per-hour), recent visitors and events.
GET /api/stats/realtime?site_id=X — Active visitors in last 5 minutes.
GET /api/stats/summary — Summary across all sites.
GET /api/stats/vitals — Live system vitals: buffer sizes and 24h aggregates.
GET /api/stats/campaigns?days=N — Campaign statistics. Optional id param for breakdown.
GET /api/stats/campaign-events?site_id=X&days=N&event_type=Y — Campaign-to-event correlation: visitors, event rate, events per visitor per campaign vs organic. event_type optional.
GET /api/stats/experiments?experiment_id=X — Experiment statistics with significance testing.

## Campaigns

GET /api/campaigns — List all UTM campaigns.
POST /api/campaigns — Create campaign. Body: { id, name, utm_campaign, site_id }. Returns 201.
PATCH /api/campaigns/:id — Update status: "active" | "paused" | "archived".
DELETE /api/campaigns/:id — Delete campaign.

## Experiments

GET /api/experiments — List all A/B experiments with variants.
POST /api/experiments — Create experiment. Body: { experiment_id, name, metric_event_type, variants: [...] }. Returns 201.
PATCH /api/experiments/:id — Update status: "draft" | "active" | "paused" | "completed" | "archived".

## Bot Management

GET /api/bots/suspicious?site_id=X&min_score=25&limit=50 — List suspicious visitors.
GET /api/bots/excluded — List excluded visitors.
POST /api/bots/exclude — Exclude visitor. Body: { visitor_id, label }. Returns 201.
DELETE /api/bots/exclude/:id — Remove exclusion.
GET /api/bots/visitor/:id — Detailed visitor data (last 100 views and events).

## Performance Metrics

GET /api/perf?category=X&metric=X&limit=100&since=ISO8601 — Query metrics.
POST /api/perf — Ingest metrics. Body: [{ category, metric, value, unit }].
GET /api/perf/trends?limit=30&since=ISO8601 — Performance trends.

## Sites

GET /api/sites/:id — Get site settings (consent CSS).
PATCH /api/sites/:id — Update site consent CSS (max 4096 chars).

## Data Ingest

POST /api/ingest — Bulk ingest visitor views and events. Max 1 MB body.

## Error Codes

400 — Missing or invalid parameters
401 — Unauthorized
403 — Forbidden (CSRF failure or PUBLIC_MODE write attempt: { error: "read_only" })
404 — Resource not found
409 — Conflict (duplicate resource)
413 — Request body too large
429 — Rate limited

---

# Bot Defense

## Overview

Every incoming request is scored from 0 (definitely human) to 100 (definitely bot). Views and events with bot_score >= 50 are excluded from daily rollups. Known bot User-Agents are dropped entirely before scoring.

## Known Bot UA Filter

Requests matching any pattern in ECHELON_BOT_UA_PATTERNS are dropped before scoring. Default list includes:

- Googlebot, Bingbot, Yandex, Baidu, DuckDuckBot
- GPTBot, ClaudeBot, ChatGPT-User, Google-Extended
- curl, wget, python-requests, HeadlessChrome, Puppeteer, Playwright

## Bot Scoring Factors

| Signal | Condition | Points |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| CF verified bot | Cloudflare confirms known bot UA | +15 |
| CF bot score <= 2 | Very confident bot | +50 |
| CF bot score 3-29 | Likely automated | +30 |
| CF bot score 30-50 | Uncertain | +10 |
| Interaction < 850ms | Beacon fired too fast | +20 |
| Interaction 850-1000ms | Suspiciously fast | +8 |
| Suspect country | IP in ECHELON_SUSPECT_COUNTRIES | +30 |
| Per-site suspect country | IP in ECHELON_SITE_SUSPECT_COUNTRIES | +30 (stacks) |
| Burst detection | > 15 requests in 5-min window | +25 |
| Missing Accept-Language | No header present | +10 |
| Missing Sec-CH-UA + Sec-Fetch-Site | Both headers absent | +10 |
| Unrealistic screen | Width/height <= 0 or > 10000 | +10 |
| No referrer + deep path | Direct visit to path with >= 2 segments | +5 |
| PoW token missing | No proof-of-work token sent | +15 |
| PoW token invalid | Token fails verification | +25 |
| PoW token replayed | Previously-used nonce | +40 |

Score is capped at 100.

## Proof-of-Work System

Every request for /ea.js embeds a WebAssembly blob and a challenge string. The browser must solve the challenge before sending beacons.

- A WASM module is generated from a 64-byte random seed every 6 hours
- Implements a SipHash-inspired algorithm with randomized constants
- Challenge string rotates every minute (HMAC-SHA256 of minute bucket)
- Client computes: token = wasm.solve(challenge + ":" + sessionId + ":" + siteId)
- Token is a 32-character hex string
- Cached in sessionStorage, re-solved on 10% of requests
- WASM solve is awaited up to 150ms before sending the beacon

Server verification tries all combinations of:

- Current + previous WASM slots (covers 6-hour rotation)
- Last N minute buckets (configurable via ECHELON_CHALLENGE_WINDOW_MINUTES, default 10)

Returns "valid", "missing", "invalid", or "replayed" — each adding different penalty points. Used nonces are tracked in memory and replaying a token adds +40 points instead of passing.

## Burst Detection

- Window: 5 minutes per IP
- Threshold: 15 requests triggers +25 penalty
- Map size: max 100,000 entries (pruned when exceeded)

## Rate Limiting

Tracking endpoints (/b.gif, /e) are rate-limited per IP:

- Default: 100 requests per 60-second window
- Configurable via ECHELON_RATE_LIMIT_MAX and ECHELON_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS

## Cloudflare Integration

Set ECHELON_BEHIND_CLOUDFLARE=true to enable:

- Read cf-ipcountry for geo data (ignores XX and T1)
- Read Cloudflare bot score from cf-bot-score header
- Detect CF-verified bots
- Use cf-connecting-ip for real client IP

## Referrer Classification

| Type | Domains |
|------|---------|
| ai | perplexity.ai, chat.openai.com, chatgpt.com, claude.ai, you.com, phind.com, copilot.microsoft.com, gemini.google.com, poe.com |
| search | Google (22 TLDs), bing.com, yahoo.com, duckduckgo.com, yandex.com/ru, ecosia.org |
| social | facebook.com, twitter.com, x.com, reddit.com, linkedin.com, instagram.com, t.co |
| direct_or_unknown | Everything else |

## Manual Exclusion

POST /api/bots/exclude — { "visitor_id": "abc123", "label": "Known bot" }
DELETE /api/bots/exclude/abc123 — Re-include visitor

## Discard Threshold

Set ECHELON_BOT_DISCARD_THRESHOLD to a score (e.g., 80) to discard high-score requests before storage. Default is 0 (store all, filter at rollup time).

---

# Configuration

## Core

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_PORT | 1947 | HTTP listen port |
| ECHELON_DB_PATH | ./echelon.db | Path to SQLite database file |
| ECHELON_SECRET | (empty) | Bearer token for API auth. If unset, PoW tokens won't survive restarts |
| ECHELON_RETENTION_DAYS | 90 | Days to keep raw visitor_views and semantic_events |

## Authentication

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_USERNAME | (empty) | Username for password-based login |
| ECHELON_PASSWORD_HASH | (empty) | PBKDF2 hash (pbkdf2$600000$<salt>$<hash>) |

Generate a password hash:

```
deno eval "import{hashPassword}from'./lib/auth.ts';console.log(await hashPassword('yourpassword'))"
```

## Bot Defense

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_SUSPECT_COUNTRIES | CN | Comma-separated ISO-2 codes that add penalty points |
| ECHELON_SUSPECT_POINTS | 30 | Points added per suspect country match |
| ECHELON_SITE_SUSPECT_COUNTRIES | (empty) | Per-site suspect countries: site:CC,CC;site:CC |
| ECHELON_BOT_DISCARD_THRESHOLD | 0 | Scores >= this are discarded before storage. 0 = store all |
| ECHELON_BOT_UA_PATTERNS | (long list) | Comma-separated UA substrings to drop entirely |
| ECHELON_CHALLENGE_WINDOW_MINUTES | 10 | Minutes of past PoW challenges accepted as valid |

## Rate Limiting

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_RATE_LIMIT_MAX | 100 | Max requests per IP per window |
| ECHELON_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS | 60000 | Rate limit window in milliseconds |

## Buffered Writes

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_VIEW_FLUSH_MS | 15000 | Flush interval for pageview buffer (ms) |
| ECHELON_EVENT_FLUSH_MS | 10000 | Flush interval for event buffer (ms) |

## Network & Proxy

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_TRUST_PROXY | false | Trust X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP for client IP |
| ECHELON_BEHIND_CLOUDFLARE | false | Trust Cloudflare headers (cf-*) for geo and bot data |
| ECHELON_TRUST_GEO_HEADERS | false | Trust cloudfront-viewer-country / x-country-code |
| ECHELON_ALLOWED_ORIGINS | (empty) | Comma-separated domains allowed to load the tracker. Empty = all |

## Admin UI

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_LIVE_STATS_MINUTES | 10 | Time window (minutes) for live stats in the admin nav bar |
| ECHELON_DISPLAY_TIMEZONE | UTC | IANA timezone for admin UI timestamps (e.g. Europe/Oslo). Data stored in UTC |

## Miscellaneous

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| ECHELON_COOKIE_CONSENT | false | Show consent banner before setting visitor cookie |
| ECHELON_IGNORED_SITES | (empty) | Comma-separated site IDs to silently ignore. "smoke-test" always ignored |
| ECHELON_PUBLIC_MODE | false | Read-only public dashboard (no auth, mutations blocked) |
| ECHELON_ANONYMIZE_SITES | (empty) | Comma-separated site IDs to anonymize in API responses |
| ECHELON_TELEMETRY | (per-instance) | Override telemetry opt-in: "true" or "false" |
| ECHELON_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS | 60000 | Graceful shutdown timeout in ms for flushing buffers |

## Example .env

```
ECHELON_PORT=1947
ECHELON_DB_PATH=./data/echelon.db
ECHELON_SECRET=change-me-to-a-secure-random-string
ECHELON_RETENTION_DAYS=90
ECHELON_USERNAME=admin
ECHELON_PASSWORD_HASH=pbkdf2$600000$...
ECHELON_TRUST_PROXY=true
ECHELON_BEHIND_CLOUDFLARE=true
ECHELON_SUSPECT_COUNTRIES=CN
ECHELON_SUSPECT_POINTS=30
ECHELON_RATE_LIMIT_MAX=100
ECHELON_LIVE_STATS_MINUTES=10
ECHELON_DISPLAY_TIMEZONE=Europe/Oslo
ECHELON_PUBLIC_MODE=false
ECHELON_ANONYMIZE_SITES=demo-site
ECHELON_TELEMETRY=true
```

---

# Testing

## Overview

236 server-side tests and 14 browser E2E tests. Tests are integrated into `deno task check` and run automatically before every tagged release via scripts/tag-release.sh.

## Running Tests

```
deno task test       # Server-side tests (unit + integration, excludes e2e)
deno task test:e2e   # Browser E2E tests (requires Chromium)
deno task check      # Full pipeline: fmt + lint + typecheck + tests
```

## Test Structure

Tests live in echelon-analytics/tests/:

| File | Module Under Test | Cases |
|------|-------------------|-------|
| _helpers.ts | Shared test infrastructure (in-memory DB, fixture inserters) | — |
| bot-score_test.ts | lib/bot-score.ts — UA detection, scoring, burst, PoW penalties | 44 |
| anonymize_test.ts | lib/anonymize.ts — HMAC hashing, fictional names, determinism | 25 |
| stats_test.ts | lib/stats.ts — overview, realtime, campaigns, experiments, dashboard | 16 |
| public-mode_test.ts | Cross-cutting PUBLIC_MODE lockdown and CSRF logic | 16 |
| events-endpoint_test.ts | routes/e.ts + lib/events-endpoint.ts — event POST, validation | 13 |
| session_test.ts | lib/session.ts — create, get, delete, expiry, prune | 12 |
| db-adapter_test.ts | lib/db/sqlite-adapter.ts — query, run, transactions, rollback | 12 |
| api-middleware_test.ts | routes/api/_middleware.ts — auth, CSRF, PUBLIC_MODE, health bypass | 12 |
| root-middleware_test.ts | routes/_middleware.ts — CORS, origin validation, referrer checks | 11 |
| challenge_test.ts | lib/challenge.ts — tokenPenalty, generateChallenge, verifyToken | 11 |
| request-stats_test.ts | lib/request-stats.ts — recordRequest, getRequestStats | 10 |
| maintenance_test.ts | lib/maintenance.ts — rollup, purge, bot exclusion, idempotency | 9 |
| config_test.ts | lib/config.ts — constantTimeEquals, validateSiteId | 8 |
| cookie_test.ts | lib/cookie.ts — getCookie, getRequestCookie | 8 |
| beacon_test.ts | routes/b.gif.ts + lib/beacon.ts — pixel beacon, bot scoring, SPA | 8 |
| buffered-writer_test.ts | lib/buffered-writer.ts — push, flush, retry, overflow | 7 |
| pagination_test.ts | lib/pagination.ts — offset, clamping, totalPages | 6 |
| sitemap_test.ts | routes/sitemap.xml.ts — admin URLs in public mode | 3 |
| robots_test.ts | routes/robots.txt.ts — public vs non-public mode | 3 |
| rate-limit_test.ts | lib/rate-limit.ts — isRateLimited | 2 |

## E2E Tests

Browser tests use @astral/astral (Deno-native headless Chromium). They live in tests/e2e/ and are excluded from the default test task since Docker builds typically lack Chromium.

| File | What It Tests |
|------|---------------|
| tracker_test.ts | Script loading, PoW challenge, page rendering |
| admin-nav_test.ts | Navigation elements, theme selector, links |
| islands_test.ts | Campaign, experiment, bot, settings page rendering |
| public-mode_test.ts | Hidden mutation controls, blocked API writes |

## Test Isolation

- Each test file gets a fresh in-memory SQLite database via createTestDb()
- Module-level state (sessions, rate limiters) uses unique keys or sequential execution
- Config constants read at import time — tests simulate logic rather than re-importing with different env vars

---

# Architecture

## Overview

Echelon Analytics is a Deno + Fresh application backed by a single SQLite database. All state — sessions, rate limiter, burst maps, buffered writers — lives in memory. Runs as a single process.

## Tech Stack

- Runtime: Deno 2.x
- Framework: Fresh 2.2.0 (file-system routing, Preact islands)
- Build: Vite 7
- CSS: Tailwind CSS v4
- UI: Preact + @preact/signals
- Database: SQLite via node:sqlite (WAL mode)
- Container: Docker (multi-stage, non-root, tini)

## Request Flow

1. Browser fetches /ea.js → dynamically-generated tracker with embedded WASM blob (rotates every 6h) and per-minute challenge string.
2. Tracker initializes: session ID from sessionStorage, UTM params from URL, starts WASM solver, attaches interaction listeners, 800ms minimum gate.
3. On user interaction → loads /b.gif with query params (path, site, session, PoW token, screen, UTM, referrer).
4. Server: rate limit check → bot UA filter → visitor identity (HMAC hash or cookie) → bot scoring (0-100) → PoW verification → data enrichment (geo, device, OS, referrer) → push to BufferedWriter.
5. BufferedWriter batches up to 50k records, flushes to SQLite every 10-15s in a single transaction.
6. Behavioral events (scroll, clicks, bounces, sessions, vitals, custom) sent via sendBeacon to POST /e → same pipeline, separate writer.

## Database

SQLite with WAL mode. PRAGMAs: journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000, foreign_keys=ON.

### Core Tables

- visitor_views: Raw pageviews with bot scores, device, OS, country, referrer, UTM. Retention: 90 days.
- visitor_views_daily: Rollup aggregates (filtered: bot_score < 50, not excluded). Retention: 730 days. Composite PK: (site_id, date, device_type, country_code, is_returning).
- semantic_events: Behavioral events with experiment/campaign linkage. Retention: 90 days.

### Supporting Tables

- excluded_visitors: Manual bot exclusion list.
- experiments / experiment_variants: A/B experiment definitions.
- utm_campaigns: Campaign tracking.
- site_settings: Per-site consent CSS.
- perf_metrics: CI/CD performance tracking.
- maintenance_log: Rollup status tracking.

## Data Pipeline

### Buffered Writers

Two BufferedWriter<T> instances:
- viewWriter: visitor_views, 15s flush, 50k max buffer.
- eventWriter: semantic_events, 10s flush, 50k max buffer.

Failed flushes re-add records to buffer. Graceful shutdown flushes remaining buffers.

### Daily Rollup (03:00 UTC)

1. Aggregate yesterday's visitor_views into visitor_views_daily.
2. Filter: bot_score >= 50 excluded, excluded_visitors removed.
3. Group by: site, date, device type, country, returning status.
4. INSERT OR REPLACE (idempotent on composite PK).
5. Purge expired data (90 days raw, 730 days rollup).
6. PRAGMA incremental_vacuum(100).

## Visitor Identity

- Cookieless (default): HMAC-SHA256(IP + UA + siteId + date) → 16-char hex. Resets daily.
- Cookie (opt-in): HttpOnly _ev cookie, 16-char hex, 30-day TTL. Enabled with data-cookie.

## Authentication & Security

- Bearer token: ECHELON_SECRET env var, constant-time comparison.
- Password: PBKDF2-SHA256, 600k iterations, 256-bit key, 128-bit salt. Web Crypto API only.
- Sessions: In-memory, random UUID tokens, 24h TTL, 30-min idle timeout, pruned every 30 minutes.
- CSRF: Mutating requests with cookie auth require Origin/Referer host to match request Host.
- PoW nonce tracking: Used tokens tracked in-memory, replay attempts penalized (+40 bot score).
- Response headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy.
- Login rate limiting: 5 attempts per IP per 15-minute window.

## Directory Structure

```
echelon-analytics/
  main.ts                  Entry point, graceful shutdown
  lib/
    tracker.ts             Tracker script generation
    beacon.ts              Pageview beacon handler
    events-endpoint.ts     Event handler
    bot-score.ts           Bot scoring heuristics
    challenge.ts           PoW challenge management
    challenge-wasm.ts      WASM bytecode builder
    buffered-writer.ts     Generic batch writer
    auth.ts                PBKDF2 hashing
    session.ts             In-memory sessions
    config.ts              Env var centralization
    maintenance.ts         Daily rollup and purge
    stats.ts               Stats queries

    admin-stats.ts         Live stats queries
    format.ts              Date/time formatting
    rate-limit.ts          Per-IP rate limiter
    db/
      adapter.ts           Database interface
      sqlite-adapter.ts    SQLite implementation
      database.ts          Init, migrations, singleton
      schema.ts            DDL and indexes
  routes/
    ea.js.ts               GET /ea.js
    b.gif.ts               GET /b.gif
    e.ts                   POST /e
    _middleware.ts          CORS, security headers
    api/                   REST API routes
    admin/                 Admin UI routes
  islands/                 Preact interactive components
  components/              Server-only Preact components
  tests/
    _helpers.ts            Test DB factory, fixture inserters
    _e2e_helpers.ts        Browser harness (Astral/Chromium)
    *_test.ts              Server-side test files
    e2e/                   Browser E2E tests
```

---

# MCP Server

## Overview

Echelon Analytics includes a read-only Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that lets AI agents (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, etc.) query analytics data directly. The server calls the Echelon REST API over HTTP — it never touches the database directly. Works with any instance: local, remote, Docker, or cloud.

## Quick Start

```
# Public instance (no token needed)
ECHELON_URL=https://ea.islets.app deno task mcp

# Local instance with authentication
ECHELON_URL=http://localhost:1947 ECHELON_SECRET=your-token deno task mcp
```

## How It Works

1. Set ECHELON_URL to point at any Echelon Analytics instance
2. The MCP server starts and connects to the AI agent via stdio (JSON-RPC)
3. When you ask a question, the AI agent picks the right MCP tool
4. The MCP server calls the corresponding GET /api/* endpoint (read-only — never POST, PATCH, or DELETE)
5. The API response is returned to the agent, which summarizes it for you

For authenticated instances, set ECHELON_SECRET — the server passes it as a Bearer token for read-only access. The server only ever calls GET endpoints; it cannot create, modify, or delete any data even if the token has write privileges. For PUBLIC_MODE instances (like the public demo), no token is needed.

## Available Tools

| Tool | Description | Parameters |
|------|-------------|------------|
| analytics_overview | Site overview: visits, uniques, top paths, devices, countries, referrers, daily trend | site_id (required), days (1-730, default 30) |
| analytics_realtime | Active visitors and top pages in last 5 minutes | site_id (required) |
| analytics_campaigns | UTM campaign stats with view/visitor counts | days (1-90, default 30), campaign_id (optional) |
| analytics_campaign_detail | Campaign breakdown by source, medium, content, term, landing pages | campaign_id (required), days (1-90, default 30) |
| analytics_experiments | A/B experiment results with conversion rates and statistical significance | experiment_id (optional) |
| analytics_campaign_events | Campaign-to-event correlation: visitors, event-triggering visitors, event rate, events per visitor, event type breakdown per campaign vs organic | site_id (required), days (1-90, default 30), event_type (optional) |
| analytics_dashboard | Live dashboard: active visitors, hourly/daily trends, recent visitors/events | site_id (required) |
| list_campaigns | List all registered UTM campaigns with IDs, names, utm_campaign values, site IDs, and statuses | (none) |
| list_experiments | List all A/B experiments with IDs, names, statuses, metric event types, and variant definitions | (none) |

All 9 tools are read-only. The server only calls GET endpoints — it cannot create, modify, or delete any data.

## Client Setup

### Claude Code (auto-discovery)

The repository includes .claude/settings.json which registers the MCP server automatically. Edit the env block to point at your instance:

```
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "echelon-analytics": {
      "command": "deno",
      "args": ["task", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "echelon-analytics",
      "env": {
        "ECHELON_URL": "https://your-instance.example.com",
        "ECHELON_SECRET": "your-api-token"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Claude Desktop

Add to ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (macOS), %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json (Windows), or ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (Linux):

```
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "echelon-analytics": {
      "command": "deno",
      "args": ["task", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "/path/to/echelon-analytics",
      "env": {
        "ECHELON_URL": "https://your-instance.example.com",
        "ECHELON_SECRET": "your-api-token"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Other MCP Clients

Any MCP-compatible client can connect via stdio transport. Set ECHELON_URL (and optionally ECHELON_SECRET) as environment variables. JSON-RPC on stdout, logs on stderr.

## Configuration

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| ECHELON_URL | Yes | Base URL of the Echelon Analytics instance (e.g. https://ea.islets.app) |
| ECHELON_SECRET | No | Bearer token for read-only API access. Not needed for PUBLIC_MODE instances. |

## Architecture

- API-backed: calls GET /api/* endpoints over HTTP — never touches the database directly
- Works with any instance (local, remote, Docker, cloud)
- Respects PUBLIC_MODE, anonymization, and access controls
- Read-only by design: only calls GET endpoints, cannot create, modify, or delete any data even with a write-capable token. This is a deliberate constraint to maintain data authenticity while learning MCP usage patterns.
- Stdio transport (JSON-RPC)
- Dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, zod

---

Source: https://github.com/janit/ea
